factors.summary: Negative impact on operating cash flows expected in the second half due to supplier commitments from a large non-U.S. contract.

evidence.text: Deposits received in the second half of 2014 related to a large non-U.S. contract awarded in our Combat Systems group are being utilized to fund supplier commitments on the program, which is expected to negatively affect operating cash flows in the second half of 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Negative impact on operating cash flows expected in the second half.

factors.summary: Government defense budgets are stable with consistent contract awards.

evidence.text: The funded portion of this backlog includes items that have been authorized and appropriated by Congress... Each of our defense groups received notable contract awards during the second quarter of 2015.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Government defense budgets are stable with consistent contract awards.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are expected to be 2% of revenues, showing a cautious but stable approach.

evidence.text: We expect capital expenditures of approximately 2 percent of revenues in 2015.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Capital expenditures are expected to remain steady at 2% of revenues.

factors.summary: The company is deleveraging with cash and equivalents decreasing but net cash position is down from prior period.

evidence.text: Our net cash position... was $619 at the end of the second quarter of 2015, down from $977 at the end of 2014.
evidence.subsection: FINANCIAL CONDITION, LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Net cash position decreased, but the impact is mixed.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are maintained with increased buybacks and dividends.

evidence.text: In 2015, we repurchased approximately 12.1 million of our outstanding shares for $1.7 billion... Cash dividends paid were $432 in the first six months of 2015, compared with $411 in the same period in 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased buybacks and dividends indicate strong shareholder returns.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving with net cash provided by operating activities increasing year-over-year.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was 92% of earnings from continuing operations in 2015 compared to 104% in 2014.
evidence.subsection: NON-GAAP FINANCIAL MEASURES – FREE CASH FLOW

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow is improving with net cash provided by operating activities increasing year-over-year.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, such as the Aerospace group, which saw margins expand due to cost reductions and improved performance.

evidence.text: Operating margins expanded 100 basis points in the second quarter and 110 basis points in the first six months of 2015 largely due to improved performance and cost-reduction efforts in the Combat Systems and Information Systems and Technology groups.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments with margin expansion.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, driven by increased deliveries and higher activity across multiple business groups.

evidence.text: We realized top-line revenue growth in the second quarter and first six months of 2015 compared with the prior-year periods. The growth was primarily due to additional deliveries of large-cabin aircraft in our Aerospace group and higher activity throughout our Marine Systems and Information Systems and Technology groups.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating due to increased deliveries and higher activity across business groups.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in the Aerospace group, with increased Gulfstream aircraft deliveries.

evidence.text: Gulfstream aircraft deliveries (in units) increased in both the second quarter and first six months of 2015 compared to the prior year periods.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in the Aerospace group, with increased Gulfstream aircraft deliveries.

factors.summary: The Marine Systems and Information Systems and Technology groups are experiencing the fastest growth, while the Combat Systems group is also showing positive momentum with increased international military vehicle contracts.

evidence.text: Revenues were stronger in all of our business groups in the first six months of 2015 compared to the similar period of 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Multiple groups are experiencing growth, including increased international military vehicle contracts.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding, driven by improved performance and cost-reduction efforts across several business groups.

evidence.text: Operating margins expanded 100 basis points in the second quarter and 110 basis points in the first six months of 2015 largely due to improved performance and cost-reduction efforts in the Combat Systems and Information Systems and Technology groups.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding significantly due to improved performance and cost reductions.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, particularly in the Combat Systems group, which saw significant margin improvements after restructuring.

evidence.text: Operating margins were up primarily due to strong operating performance and reduced overhead costs following restructuring activities completed in 2014 and additional cost cutting across the portfolio.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, especially in the Combat Systems group.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, particularly in the Combat Systems group, which saw cost reductions and efficiency gains.

evidence.text: Operating margins were up primarily due to strong operating performance and reduced overhead costs following restructuring activities completed in 2014 and additional cost cutting across the portfolio.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improving productivity in the Combat Systems group with cost reductions and efficiency gains.

factors.summary: Management has maintained forward guidance, expecting continued growth in 2015 with revenue increases and margin expansions across several business groups.

evidence.text: We expect an increase in the group's full-year revenues of approximately 6 percent in 2015 compared with 2014 as a result of increased Gulfstream aircraft deliveries. Operating margins are expected to be in the mid- 18 percent range.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management expects continued growth with revenue increases and margin expansions.

factors.summary: International sales are growing, particularly in the Combat Systems group, with increased activity on international contracts.

evidence.text: Revenues from international military vehicles decreased in the second quarter of 2015 due to lower revenues on several mature international contracts that are nearing completion and new contracts that are beginning.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: International sales have mixed results with decreased revenues on mature contracts but new activity.

factors.summary: Demand trends are generally positive, with accelerating demand across certain groups and stable contract awards in others. The Aerospace group saw significant growth due to increased aircraft deliveries, while Marine Systems and Information Systems also contributed to revenue growth. Defense groups maintained stable demand with consistent contract awards.

evidence.text: Each of our defense groups received notable contract awards during the second quarter of 2015.

We realized top-line revenue growth in the second quarter and first six months of 2015 compared with the prior-year periods. The growth was primarily due to additional deliveries of large-cabin aircraft in our Aerospace group and higher activity throughout our Marine Systems and Information Systems and Technology groups.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends are positive across groups with accelerating growth and stable contract awards.

factors.summary: Management confidence is evident as the company has demonstrated increased buybacks and dividends, along with a positive outlook across multiple business groups. The successful execution of cost reduction initiatives further supports this confidence.

evidence.text: In 2015, we repurchased approximately 12.1 million of our outstanding shares for $1.7 billion... Cash dividends paid were $432 in the first six months of 2015, compared with $411 in the same period in 2014.

We expect the Combat Systems group’s 2015 revenues to be generally consistent with 2014 as growth on our international military vehicle contracts offsets some scheduled reductions in spending on several U.S. military production programs. Operating margins are expected to be in the mid-15 percent range.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company's increased buybacks, dividends, and positive outlook across business groups indicate strong management confidence.

factors.summary: The backlog orders are mixed across different groups. While the Defense Groups overall saw a slight decrease in total backlog, the Information Systems and Technology group experienced an increase. In contrast, the Aerospace group showed growth with a book-to-bill ratio exceeding one-to-one.

evidence.text: Total backlog in our defense groups was $56 billion on July 5, 2015, compared with $57.4 billion on April 5, 2015. Backlog increased in our Information Systems and Technology group, while Combat Systems and Marine Systems backlog was down slightly as the groups perform on major, multi-year contracts awarded in 2014.
Aerospace book-to-bill (orders divided by revenues) was in excess of one-to-one in the second quarter of 2015.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Backlog orders show mixed trends with some groups increasing while others decrease, indicating a neutral sentiment.

factors.summary: The overall contract backlog shows mixed trends across different groups. While the defense groups report a stable backlog with slight variations, the Aerospace group experiences a notable increase in both funded and unfunded backlog.

evidence.text: Total backlog in our defense groups was $56 billion on July 5, 2015, compared with $57.4 billion on April 5, 2015. Backlog increased in our Information Systems and Technology group, while Combat Systems and Marine Systems backlog was down slightly...

Aerospace funded backlog represents aircraft orders for which we have definitive purchase contracts and deposits from customers. Unfunded backlog consists of agreements to provide future aircraft maintenance and support services. The group ended the second quarter of 2015 with backlog of $14 billion , up 7 percent from $13.1 billion on April 5, 2015 .

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The overall contract backlog shows stability and growth in certain groups, suggesting a positive sentiment.
